PSI-I.summary: Instinkt describes itself as the oldest mind, fear/feeling-based, protective, distrustful, attached to safety and close people, with flight as primary defense and attack only when cornered.

PSI-E.summary: Emocio describes itself as image-based, optimistic, social, competitive, pleasure/beauty/body oriented, with attack as defense and difficulty releasing desired images.

PSI-R.summary: Racio describes itself as the youngest mind, analytical, verbal, numerical, structured, planning-oriented, conscious, and capable of translating Emocio and Instinkt.

PSI-EGO-DEL1.summary: Opening Eros-Life dialogue: the world a person experiences is created by three minds; Ego lives in that world and mistakes it for reality.

PSI-EGO-DEL2.summary: Continuation: all three minds are equivalent, Ego can trust one/two/three differently, twelve plus one character structures emerge, and no single mind owns the truth.

EROS-KDO-POSLUSA-BESEDE.summary: Eros states that only Racio listens to words; Emocio and Instinkt observe/perceive other signals.

EROS-ZAZNAVANJA-E-I.summary: Emocio and Instinkt perceive more than Racio in different channels; Instinkt is linked to smell/taste, Emocio to visual patterning.

EROS-FOBIJE.summary: Fear/fobias are linked to Instinkt; Racio talking does not directly reach Instinkt's non-verbal fear language.

EROS-KAKO-POSTANEMO-JEZNI.summary: Anger often arises when Emocio's desired image cannot become current reality; Racio may later invent an explanation.

EROS-NAPAKE-PRI-DOLOCANJU.summary: The key is not merely what a person does but how a thought or action was produced; the same behavior can have different processor origins.

EROS-VSAK-CLOVEK-ZIVI-V-SVOJEM-SVETU.summary: Each person lives in an individual world largely created by their leading processor; the same reality is processed differently.
